acceptance_targets: [RT_DOCUMENT_INTERIORITY_DROP] In the revised passage anchored around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:93, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:103, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:109, the reader can track Cliff reading in real time rather than absorbing a stand-alone synopsis. Pass only if the section contains at least three distinct Cliff filters inside the RT account and no 10-plus-paragraph run remains without Cliff thought, sensation, or self-assessment.

[RT_DOCUMENT_DATE_HORIZON_SEAM] The revised RT packet passes only if every official detail in the lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:101 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:105 clearly belongs to the date logic established at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:87 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:89. It fails if the same dated note still reads as both hot confession and post-investigation summary without the text marking the difference.
[RED-02] Ch 8 no longer contains a passage stating Tommy's age, occupation, and family status together as a single expository block.
[CON-02] Cliff's estimate and Paul's exact count are compatible — both describe the same amount as either just over or just under two thousand.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage anchored around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:93, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:103, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:109, the reader can track Cliff reading in real time rather than absorbing a stand-alone synopsis. Pass only if the section contains at least three distinct Cliff filters inside the RT account and no 10-plus-paragraph run remains without Cliff thought, sensation, or self-assessment.
findings.problem: The chapter's defining discovery drops Cliff's close-third filter for too long. After the brief anchor at line 91, the RT section runs as a ten-paragraph backstory dossier before Cliff's interior reaction returns at line 113, so the reveal lands as information transfer more than lived discovery.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the RT-reading sequence at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:85, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:91, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:93, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:103, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:109 so the document remains the source of the facts but the scene stays inside Cliff. Keep the core fire facts, but interrupt the lines 93-111 run with brief Cliff-side processing: a bodily reaction, a legal or financial parsing instinct, and a moment where the moral temperature changes before the later leverage math.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised RT packet passes only if every official detail in the lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:101 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:105 clearly belongs to the date logic established at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:87 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:89. It fails if the same dated note still reads as both hot confession and post-investigation summary without the text marking the difference.
findings.problem: The RT document is dated August 17, 2002 and framed as an immediate memory record, but it also reads as if Martin already has later investigative conclusions in hand: a coroner's blood-alcohol result and a settled accidental-fire ruling. Because this document is the novel's motive engine, the mixed time horizon weakens the artifact's plausibility at the exact moment the reader needs to trust it as real leverage, not a plot memo.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:87, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:89, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_08.md:105, choose one documentary logic and make the text obey it. Either redate the note to a point when Martin could plausibly know those official findings, or keep the August 17 dating and move the report-level details into a clearly marked later addendum, margin note, or separate page that Cliff can still find in the envelope.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 8 no longer contains a passage stating Tommy's age, occupation, and family status together as a single expository block.
findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 43: 'Six-two and still built like something functional.' Ch 2 line 564: 'coaching shorts.' Ch 5 line 1633: 'Apple Valley football' / 'Eastview.' Ch 8 line 2446: 'Tommy Renaldi was forty-seven, a high school football coach and PE teacher, married with three kids and a public life built in a suburb where Friday nights still meant something.'
findings.problem: Ch 8 delivers a full name-plus-role-plus-family re-introduction of Tommy (age, occupation, family status, community role) as a single dossier-style block, as if the reader has not spent seven chapters with him. Tommy's coaching role was established in Chs 1, 2, 3, and confirmed in Ch 5's interview.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or sharply reduce the expository summary at line 2446. Cliff's calculation can reference what Tommy stands to lose without re-stating his full CV. Replace 'a high school football coach and PE teacher, married with three kids' with a compressed reference that assumes the reader knows Tommy.

findings.acceptance_test: Cliff's estimate and Paul's exact count are compatible — both describe the same amount as either just over or just under two thousand.
findings.evidence: Ch 8 line 2380 (Cliff POV): 'The stack was twenties and fifties, just over two thousand dollars.' Ch 11 line 3201 (Paul POV): 'Nineteen hundred and sixty dollars.'
findings.problem: Cliff and Paul each count the same clipped stack of bills in Martin's safe without removing the clip. Cliff estimates 'just over two thousand dollars' but Paul counts '$1,960.' Since $1,960 is just UNDER two thousand, Cliff's 'just over' description is contradictory. No one removes cash between the two scenes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Change Cliff's estimate at Ch 8 line 2380 from 'just over two thousand dollars' to 'close to two thousand dollars' or 'just under two thousand.' Alternatively, change Paul's count to $2,040 to match 'just over.'

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
